recipes.title: Berry Sparkler Mocktail
recipes.menu: Bright berries with sparkling juice and a citrusy fizz.
recipes.image_alt: Berry Sparkler Mocktail

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1 cup frozen mixed berries (thawed slightly)
  • 500 ml non-alcoholic sparkling grape juice or sparkling apple juice
  • 200 ml lemonade or ginger ale
  • 2-4 fresh strawberries (for garnish)
  • Ice cubes, as needed
sections.method_html:
1. Divide the thawed mixed berries between two tall glasses (about 1/2 cup per glass).
  2. Lightly muddle the berries with a spoon to release some juice, keeping them mostly whole.
  3. Fill each glass halfway with ice cubes.
  4. Pour 250 ml of sparkling grape or apple juice into each glass, then top with 100 ml of lemonade or ginger ale.
  5. Stir gently to combine. Garnish each glass with a sliced strawberry on the rim and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Caprese Salad with Balsamic Glaze
recipes.menu: Tomatoes and mozzarella with basil and balsamic.
recipes.image_alt: Caprese Salad with Balsamic Glaze

sections.ingredients_html:
• 3-4 ripe tomatoes (about 400 g), sliced into rounds
  • 200 g bocconcini or fresh mozzarella, sliced into rounds
  • 1 punnet fresh basil leaves (about 20-30 leaves)
  • 2 tbsp balsamic glaze
  • Optional: 1 tbsp pesto (for drizzling)
  • 4-6 slices sourdough or baguette (for serving)
sections.method_html:
1. On a serving platter or two plates, alternate slices of tomato and mozzarella in a circular or linear pattern.
  2. Tuck fresh basil leaves between the slices for even distribution.
  3. Drizzle evenly with the balsamic glaze (and pesto if using). Season lightly with salt and pepper.
  4. If desired, lightly toast the sourdough slices at 180 C for 5 minutes.
  5. Serve immediately with the bread on the side for scooping or dipping.

recipes.title: Charred Corn Tostadas with Avocado, Tomato & Lime
recipes.menu: Crisp tostadas with charred corn and creamy avocado give the menu a brighter, crunchier opening than the recent dairy-led starters.
recipes.image_alt: Charred Corn Tostadas with Avocado, Tomato & Lime

sections.ingredients_html:
• 4 small white corn tortillas
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 200 g sweet corn kernels
  • 1 ripe avocado
  • 150 g cherry tomatoes, finely diced
  • 2 tbsp finely diced red onion
  • 1 tbsp lime juice
  • 1 tbsp chopped coriander
  • 1/2 tsp ground cumin
  • 1/4 tsp chilli flakes
  • Flaky salt
  • Black pepper
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the oven to 220 C and brush both sides of the tortillas lightly with a little of the olive oil, then bake directly on the oven rack for 6-8 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.
  2. Heat the remaining olive oil in a skillet over high heat and cook the sweet corn with the cumin and chilli flakes for 3-4 minutes until it picks up dark charred spots.
  3. Stir the cherry tomatoes, red onion, and half the coriander into the hot corn, then season with flaky salt and black pepper.
  4. Mash the avocado with the lime juice and a pinch of flaky salt.
  5. Spread the avocado over the tostadas, spoon over the charred corn mixture, and finish with the remaining coriander before serving.

recipes.title: Chocolate Malt Mousse Sundae Cups
recipes.menu: Silky chocolate mousse with a gentle malt note lands the diner theme in dessert form without asking for any baking.
recipes.image_alt: Chocolate Malt Mousse Sundae Cups

sections.ingredients_html:
• 150 g milk cooking chocolate (mousse and garnish)
  • 250 ml thickened cream (mousse and topping)
  • 1 tbsp malted chocolate powder
  • 2 tsp caster sugar
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
  • Pinch of flaky salt
sections.method_html:
1. Put 100 ml of the thickened cream in a small saucepan and warm it just to a simmer.
  2. Tip the hot cream over the milk chocolate, add the malted chocolate powder, vanilla extract, and flaky salt, then whisk until smooth and glossy. Leave it to cool for 10 minutes.
  3. Whip the remaining thickened cream with the caster sugar to soft peaks.
  4. Fold two-thirds of the whipped cream through the cooled chocolate mixture until no streaks remain.
  5. Spoon the mousse into two glasses or sundae cups and chill for 20 minutes.
  6. Top with the remaining whipped cream, then grate a little of the reserved chocolate over each serve before taking them to the table.

recipes.title: Chocolate-Dipped Strawberries with Chantilly Cream
recipes.menu: Glossy chocolate berries with vanilla-scented cream.
recipes.image_alt: Chocolate-Dipped Strawberries with Chantilly Cream

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1 punnet fresh strawberries (about 250 g), washed and dried
  • 150 g dark or milk chocolate melts
  • 300 ml thickened cream
  • 2 tbsp icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla essence
sections.method_html:
1. Line a plate or tray with baking paper. Melt the chocolate in a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second bursts, stirring between each, until smooth.
  2. Holding each strawberry by the stem, dip halfway into the melted chocolate. Let excess drip off, then place on the prepared tray.
  3. Refrigerate the dipped strawberries for 10 minutes to set.
  4. Meanwhile, whip the thickened cream with the icing sugar and vanilla until soft peaks form.
  5. Serve the set strawberries with a dollop or piped swirl of chantilly cream on the side.

recipes.title: Coconut Sago with Lime-Glossed Pineapple
recipes.menu: Silky coconut pudding and bright tropical fruit close the menu with a light but luxe finish.
recipes.image_alt: Coconut Sago with Lime-Glossed Pineapple

sections.ingredients_html:
• 80 g sago pearls
  • 250 ml water
  • 400 ml coconut milk
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 1 pinch salt
  • 250 g pineapple, diced
  • 1 tbsp lime juice
  • 1 tsp lime zest
  • 1 tbsp toasted coconut flakes
  • 6 mint leaves
sections.method_html:
1. Rinse the sago pearls, then simmer in 250 ml water for 10 minutes, stirring often, until mostly translucent.
  2. Drain the sago, return it to the pan, add coconut milk, sugar, and salt, and cook for 3-4 minutes until glossy.
  3. In a small skillet over medium heat, cook the diced pineapple for 2-3 minutes until lightly caramelized.
  4. Add lime juice to the pineapple, toss for 30 seconds, then remove from heat.
  5. Spoon the warm coconut sago into bowls, top with lime-glossed pineapple, and finish with lime zest, toasted coconut flakes, and mint leaves.

recipes.title: Creamy Garlic Prawn Pasta
recipes.menu: Silky garlic cream with prawns and spinach.
recipes.image_alt: Creamy Garlic Prawn Pasta

sections.ingredients_html:
• 250 g raw prawns (peeled and deveined)
  • 200 g fresh fettuccine or linguine
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 300 ml thickened cream
  • 2 cups baby spinach (about 100 g)
  • 1/2 cup grated parmesan cheese, plus extra for serving
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • Optional: Pinch of chilli flakes
sections.method_html:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook according to packet instructions (usually 3-4 minutes for fresh). Drain and set aside, reserving 1/2 cup pasta water.
  2. In a large frying pan over medium heat, warm the olive oil and butter. Add the garlic (and chilli flakes if using) and saute for 1 minute until fragrant.
  3. Add the prawns and cook for 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until pink and opaque.
  4. Pour in the thickened cream and bring to a gentle simmer for 2-3 minutes to thicken slightly. Stir in the baby spinach until wilted, about 1 minute.
  5. Add the cooked pasta and parmesan to the pan. Toss to combine, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if needed. Season with salt and pepper.
  6. Divide between two bowls, top with extra parmesan, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Crispy Chicken with Lemon-Caper Butter, Parmesan Polenta & Garlicky Greens
recipes.menu: Crisp-skinned chicken, silky polenta, bright lemon-caper butter.
recipes.image_alt: Crispy Chicken with Lemon-Caper Butter, Parmesan Polenta & Garlicky Greens

sections.ingredients_html:
• 2 chicken thighs (skin-on, bone-in or boneless)
  • Salt and black pepper (for chicken, polenta, and greens)
  • 4 tsp olive oil (1 tbsp for chicken, 1 tsp for greens)
  • 30-40 g butter
  • 1 tbsp capers, drained
  • 1 lemon (zest + 2 tbsp juice)
  • 2 small garlic cloves (1 finely grated for lemon-caper butter, optional; 1 sliced for greens)
  • 1 tbsp chopped parsley (or a small pinch of chopped sage)
  • 1/2 cup instant polenta
  • 2 cups water (or 1 cup water + 1 cup milk)
  • 30 g parmesan, finely grated
  • 1 bunch broccolini or 2 handfuls baby spinach
sections.method_html:
1. Season chicken well with salt and pepper.
  2. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Place chicken skin-side down and cook until deeply golden and crisp, about 8-12 minutes (reduce heat if it's browning too fast).
  3. Flip chicken and cook through, 6-10 minutes more (boneless cooks faster). Rest on a plate, loosely covered.
  4. Polenta: Bring water (or water + milk) to a simmer. Whisk in polenta in a steady stream. Cook 2-3 minutes, stirring, until thick. Stir in parmesan, season, and cover to keep warm.
  5. Greens: In a second pan (or after chicken comes out), warm olive oil, add sliced garlic for 20-30 seconds, then add greens and cook until just tender. Season lightly.
  6. Lemon-caper butter: In the chicken pan (leave the tasty bits), reduce heat to low. Add butter, capers, lemon zest, and garlic (if using). Swirl until foamy. Add lemon juice, taste, and adjust salt.
  7. Spoon polenta onto plates, add greens, top with chicken, then drizzle over lemon-caper butter. Finish with parsley (or a touch of sage).
sections.notes_html: • Easy vegetarian swap: Replace chicken with thick slices of halloumi or large field mushrooms. Sear until golden, then use the same lemon-caper butter.

recipes.title: Crispy Halloumi with Hot Honey, Mint & Pistachio
recipes.menu: Salty-crisp halloumi with floral heat and fresh herbs for a bold but effortless first plate.
recipes.image_alt: Crispy Halloumi with Hot Honey, Mint & Pistachio

sections.ingredients_html:
• 200 g halloumi, sliced into 8 pieces
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 1/2 tsp chilli flakes
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 60 g baby rocket
  • 1 tbsp fresh mint, chopped
  • 20 g pistachios, chopped
  • Freshly cracked black pepper
sections.method_html:
1. Stir honey, chilli flakes, and lemon juice in a small bowl.
  2. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear halloumi for 2-3 minutes per side until deeply golden.
  3. Spread baby rocket on two plates and top with hot halloumi slices.
  4. Spoon over the hot honey dressing, then finish with mint, pistachios, and black pepper.

recipes.title: Dark Chocolate Tahini Pots with Orange & Pistachio
recipes.menu: Bitter chocolate, nutty tahini, and fresh orange finish the meal with a richer, more intimate dessert than the fruit-led sets around it.
recipes.image_alt: Dark Chocolate Tahini Pots with Orange & Pistachio

sections.ingredients_html:
• 120 g dark chocolate, finely chopped
  • 150 ml thickened cream
  • 2 tbsp tahini
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 1 pinch flaky salt
  • 1 orange
  • 2 tbsp pistachios, finely chopped
sections.method_html:
1. Put the dark chocolate in a heatproof bowl. Warm the thickened cream in a small saucepan until just steaming, then pour it over the chocolate.
  2. Leave for 1 minute, add the tahini, honey, and flaky salt, then whisk until smooth and glossy.
  3. Divide between two small glasses or ramekins and chill for 15 minutes until softly set.
  4. Cut away the peel from the orange and segment it over a bowl to catch any juices.
  5. Spoon the orange segments over the chocolate pots, finish with the pistachios, and serve cold.

recipes.title: Diner Basket Crinkle Fries with Dill Pickle Mayo
recipes.menu: Basket fries with sharp pickle mayo give the menu its salty-crisp side without adding much active work before the burger.
recipes.image_alt: Diner Basket Crinkle Fries with Dill Pickle Mayo

sections.ingredients_html:
• 500 g frozen crinkle cut chips
  • 1 tbsp neutral oil
  • 1/2 tsp flaky salt
  • 1/4 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp smoked paprika
  • 80 g whole-egg mayonnaise
  • 2 dill pickles, finely chopped
  • 1 tsp dill pickle brine
  • 1 tbsp finely chopped chives
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the oven to 220 C and line a tray.
  2. Toss the frozen crinkle cut chips with the neutral oil, spread them on the tray, and bake for 22-25 minutes, turning once, until deeply golden.
  3. While the fries cook, stir the mayonnaise, finely chopped dill pickles, dill pickle brine, half the chives, and the lemon juice together in a small bowl.

  5. Pile the fries into two lined bowls or one shared basket, spoon over the remaining chives, and serve with the dill pickle mayo alongside.

recipes.title: Dutch-Oven Fried Chicken Burgers with Shrettuce & Pickle Sauce
recipes.menu: A crunchy Dutch-oven fried chicken burger with cool lettuce and sharp pickle sauce makes the whole menu feel like a proper late-night diner order.
recipes.image_alt: Dutch-Oven Fried Chicken Burgers with Shrettuce & Pickle Sauce

sections.ingredients_html:
• 350 g chicken thigh fillets, trimmed and halved into 2 large pieces
  • 150 ml buttermilk
  • 4 tbsp dill pickle brine
  • 140 g plain flour
  • 40 g cornflour
  • 1 tsp smoked paprika
  • 1 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/2 tsp onion powder
  • 1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp flaky salt
  • 1/2 tsp cracked black pepper
  • 1 l vegetable oil
  • 3 tbsp whole-egg mayonnaise
  • 1 tbsp tomato sauce
  • 1 tsp American mustard
  • 1 dill pickle, finely chopped
  • 2 brioche burger buns
  • 60 g shredded iceberg lettuce
  • 6 dill pickle slices
sections.method_html:
1. Toss the chicken with the buttermilk and 2 tbsp of the dill pickle brine, then leave it to marinate for 15 minutes while you prepare the rest.
  2. In a shallow bowl, mix the plain flour, cornflour, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, baking powder, flaky salt, and black pepper. Drizzle 1 tbsp of the chicken marinade into the flour and rub it through with your fingertips so the coating forms small craggy clumps.
  3. Heat the vegetable oil in a medium Dutch oven over medium-high heat until it reaches 175 C. While it heats, stir the mayonnaise, tomato sauce, American mustard, finely chopped dill pickle, and the remaining dill pickle brine together for the sauce.
  4. Lift the chicken out of the marinade, press it firmly into the flour mixture, and fry for 3-4 minutes per side until deep golden and cooked through. Rest the chicken on a rack or paper towel for 5 minutes.
  5. Toast the brioche buns cut-side down in a dry pan until lightly golden.
  6. Spread the pickle sauce over both bun halves, pile the base buns with shredded iceberg lettuce, top with the fried chicken and dill pickle slices, then close the burgers and serve hot.

recipes.title: Espresso Tiramisu Cups (No Alcohol)
recipes.menu: Creamy mascarpone layers with espresso-soaked biscuits and cocoa.
recipes.image_alt: Espresso Tiramisu Cups (No Alcohol)

sections.ingredients_html:
• 125 g mascarpone
  • 150 ml thickened cream
  • 2 tbsp icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1/2 cup strong espresso or very strong instant coffee, cooled
  • 8-10 sponge fingers (savoiardi), broken to fit
  • 1-2 tbsp cocoa powder
  • Optional: Dark chocolate, finely grated (to finish)
sections.method_html:
1. Whip cream, icing sugar, and vanilla to soft peaks.
  2. Fold mascarpone through gently until smooth and thick.
  3. Dip sponge finger pieces quickly into cooled coffee (1-2 seconds per side).
  4. Layer coffee-dipped biscuits and mascarpone cream into two glasses. Aim for 2-3 layers.
  5. Chill for at least 30 minutes.
  6. Dust generously with cocoa just before serving. Add grated dark chocolate if you feel fancy.

recipes.title: Ginger Slice
recipes.image_alt: Ginger Slice

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1 3/4 cups flour
  • 1 cup caster sugar
  • 125 g glace ginger
  • 1 egg
  • 150--185g butter
  • 30 g blanched almonds
sections.method_html:
1. Sift flour, then add sugar, and chopped ginger.
  2. Mix in melted butter and egg. Press into well.
  3. Pour mixture into greased sandwich tin.
  4. Brush top with milk, decorate with almonds.
  5. Bake 45 minutes at 170C.
  6. Cool in tin, cut in wedges.

**Notes**

Very rich, small serves.

recipes.title: Goat Cheese Tartines with Balsamic Shallots & Thyme
recipes.menu: Creamy goat cheese and glossy balsamic shallots turn simple tartines into a proper little wine-bar starter.
recipes.image_alt: Goat Cheese Tartines with Balsamic Shallots & Thyme

sections.ingredients_html:
• 2 banana shallots, thinly sliced
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar
  • 1 tsp honey
  • 100 g goat cheese
  • 2 tbsp thickened cream
  • 4 baguette slices
  • 1 tsp thyme leaves
  • Pinch of flaky salt
  • Black pepper
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the olive oil in a small frying pan over medium heat, add the shallots and a pinch of flaky salt, and cook for 5-6 minutes until soft and lightly golden.
  2. Stir in the balsamic vinegar and honey, cook for 1 minute until glossy, then take the pan off the heat.
  3. Toast the baguette slices in a dry pan, under the grill, or in the toaster until crisp at the edges.
  4. Stir the goat cheese, thickened cream, and half the thyme together until spreadable.
  5. Spread the goat cheese mixture over the warm tartines, spoon the balsamic shallots on top, and finish with the remaining thyme and a few grinds of black pepper.
  6. Plate the tartines on a small board or side plates and serve while still slightly warm.

recipes.title: Lychee Ginger Lime Cooler
recipes.menu: Floral lychee, bright lime, and ginger spice open the night with a crisp bar-style fizz.
recipes.image_alt: Lychee Ginger Lime Cooler

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1 can lychees in syrup (reserve 80 ml syrup and 6 lychees, plus 2 extra lychees for garnish)
  • 40 ml fresh lime juice
  • 20 ml ginger syrup
  • 6 mint leaves
  • 240 ml chilled soda water
  • 2 cup ice
  • 2 strip lime peel
sections.method_html:
1. Blend 6 lychees with 80 ml syrup, lime juice, and ginger syrup until smooth.
  2. Strain into a jug, add mint leaves and ice, then stir for 20 seconds to chill.
  3. Divide between two tall glasses and top with chilled soda water.
  4. Garnish each glass with one whole lychee and a strip of lime peel.
  5. Optional cocktail conversion: add 30 ml gin or vodka per glass and stir once.

recipes.title: Miso-Butter Broccolini with Toasted Sesame Almonds
recipes.menu: Fast charred greens with nutty crunch and savory glaze for a restaurant-style small plate.
recipes.image_alt: Miso-Butter Broccolini with Toasted Sesame Almonds

sections.ingredients_html:
• 250 g broccolini
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp unsalted butter
  • 1 tbsp white miso paste
  • 1 tsp honey
  • 1 tbsp water
  • 1 tsp lime juice
  • 1 tsp lime zest
  • 20 g flaked almonds
  • 1 tbsp toasted sesame seeds
sections.method_html:
1. Heat a dry skillet over medium heat, toast the flaked almonds for 1-2 minutes, then transfer to a plate.
  2. Add olive oil to the same skillet, add broccolini, and cook for 3-4 minutes until blistered in spots.
  3. Add 1 tbsp water, cover for 1 minute, then uncover and cook until the water evaporates.
  4. Lower heat, add butter, miso paste, honey, and lime juice, and toss until the broccolini is evenly glazed.
  5. Plate the broccolini, finish with lime zest, toasted almonds, and sesame seeds, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Miso-Sesame Mushroom Lettuce Cups
recipes.menu: Savory glossy mushrooms in crisp lettuce cups deliver big umami and clean contrast with almost no cleanup.
recipes.image_alt: Miso-Sesame Mushroom Lettuce Cups

sections.ingredients_html:
• 300 g Swiss brown mushrooms, finely chopped
  • 1 tbsp neutral oil
  • 2 tbsp white miso paste
  • 1 tbsp soy sauce
  • 2 tsp rice vinegar
  • 2 tsp caster sugar
  • 1 tsp sesame oil
  • 1 garlic clove, minced

sections.method_html:

  4. Spoon the hot mushroom mixture into butter lettuce leaves.
  5. Finish with spring onions and toasted sesame seeds, then serve immediately.

recipes.title: No-Bake Lemon Mascarpone Pots with Pistachio Crunch
recipes.menu: Lush lemon mascarpone cream layered with biscuit and pistachio for a bright, elegant finish.
recipes.image_alt: No-Bake Lemon Mascarpone Pots with Pistachio Crunch

sections.ingredients_html:
• 125 g mascarpone
  • 150 ml thickened cream
  • 2 tbsp icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 tsp finely grated lemon zest
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 6 plain sweet biscuits (e.g., Marie biscuits), crushed
  • 2 tbsp chopped pistachios
  • Pinch of flaky salt
  • Optional: extra lemon zest to finish
sections.method_html:
1. Mix crushed biscuits, pistachios, and flaky salt in a small bowl.
  2. Whip cream, icing sugar, and vanilla to soft peaks.
  3. Fold mascarpone, lemon zest, and lemon juice into the whipped cream until smooth.
  4. Spoon a little biscuit mixture into two small glasses, then add a thick layer of lemon mascarpone cream.
  5. Repeat with another light biscuit layer and finish with cream on top.
  6. Chill for 20 minutes, then garnish with extra lemon zest before serving.

recipes.title: One-Pan Dijon Chicken with Leeks, Baby Potatoes & Thyme
recipes.menu: Dijon-coated chicken, sweet leeks, and buttery potatoes deliver an easy bistro main with very little washing up.
recipes.image_alt: One-Pan Dijon Chicken with Leeks, Baby Potatoes & Thyme

sections.ingredients_html:
• 450 g baby potatoes, halved
  • 1 leek, halved lengthways and sliced into thick half-moons
  • 350 g chicken thigh fillets (boneless, skinless)
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp Dijon mustard
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 1 tsp thyme leaves
  • 100 ml chicken stock
  • 80 ml thickened cream
  • 15 g butter
  • Pinch of flaky salt
  • Black pepper
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the oven to 220 C and line a roasting tray or ovenproof pan.
  2. Toss the baby potatoes and leek with half the olive oil, half the thyme, a pinch of flaky salt, and black pepper, then spread them over the tray and roast for 10 minutes.
  3. Meanwhile, toss the chicken thigh fillets with the remaining olive oil, Dijon mustard, garlic, and the rest of the thyme.
  4. Take the tray out, turn the vegetables, nestle the chicken in among them, and pour the chicken stock around the tray.
  5. Roast for 15-18 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
  6. Drizzle over the thickened cream, dot with the butter, and return to the oven for 3-4 minutes until the sauce looks glossy.
  7. Rest for 5 minutes, then spoon the chicken, leeks, potatoes, and pan sauce into shallow bowls for serving.

recipes.title: One-Pan Ginger Miso Chicken Rice with Bok Choy
recipes.menu: Comforting one-pan centerpiece with glossy umami sauce and tender greens for low-stress cooking.
recipes.image_alt: One-Pan Ginger Miso Chicken Rice with Bok Choy

sections.ingredients_html:
• 300 g chicken thigh fillets (boneless, skinless), sliced
  • 1 tbsp neutral oil
  • 2 clove garlic, minced
  • 1 tsp grated ginger
  • 1 tbsp white miso paste
  • 1 tbsp soy sauce
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 180 g jasmine rice
  • 430 ml chicken stock
  • 2 baby bok choy, halved lengthways
  • 1 tsp sesame oil
  • 1 spring onion, finely sliced
  • 1 tbsp toasted sesame seeds
sections.method_html:
1. Heat a deep skillet with neutral oil over medium-high heat and brown the chicken for 3-4 minutes.
  2. Stir in garlic and grated ginger for 30 seconds, then add miso paste, soy sauce, and honey.
  3. Add jasmine rice and chicken stock, scrape the pan base, and bring to a gentle boil.
  4. Nestle the chicken evenly into the rice, cover, and cook on low heat for 12 minutes.
  5. Place bok choy on top, cover again, and cook for 4 more minutes until the rice is tender and bok choy is bright.
  6. Rest the pan off heat for 3 minutes, then finish with sesame oil, spring onion, and sesame seeds before serving.

recipes.title: One-Pan Lemon Parmesan Gnocchi with Spinach
recipes.menu: Golden pan-seared gnocchi in a silky lemon-parmesan sauce with wilted spinach and crispy sage.
recipes.image_alt: One-Pan Lemon Parmesan Gnocchi with Spinach

sections.ingredients_html:
• 500 g shelf-stable potato gnocchi
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 30 g butter
  • 8-10 fresh sage leaves
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 1 cup low-sodium vegetable stock
  • 3/4 cup thickened cream
  • 1 lemon (zest + 2 tbsp juice)
  • 1/2 cup finely grated parmesan, plus extra to finish
  • 2 cups baby spinach
  • Salt and black pepper
  • Optional: pinch of chili flakes
sections.method_html:
1. Heat a large frying pan over medium heat with butter until foamy, add sage leaves, and fry for 30-45 seconds until crisp; transfer leaves to a plate.
  2. Add olive oil and gnocchi to the same pan and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly golden.
  3. Add garlic (and chili flakes if using) and stir for 30 seconds.
  4. Pour in stock and cream, then simmer for 3-4 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
  5. Stir in lemon zest, lemon juice, parmesan, and spinach; cook for 1-2 minutes until spinach wilts and sauce is glossy.
  6. Season to taste, divide between bowls, and top with crispy sage, extra parmesan, and black pepper.

recipes.title: One-Pan Saffron Prawn Orzo with Spinach
recipes.menu: Golden saffron orzo with tender prawns and greens delivers a luxe one-pan centerplate with minimal cleanup.
recipes.image_alt: One-Pan Saffron Prawn Orzo with Spinach

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 180 g orzo
  • 300 ml vegetable stock
  • 200 ml water
  • 1 pinch saffron threads
  • 250 g raw prawns, peeled and deveined
  • 120 g cherry tomatoes, halved
  • 60 g baby spinach
  • 40 g parmesan, finely grated
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
  • Freshly cracked black pepper
sections.method_html:
1. Warm the stock with saffron in a small pot for 2 minutes, then keep it hot.
  2. Heat olive oil in a deep skillet over medium heat and cook garlic for 30 seconds.
  3. Add orzo and stir for 1 minute, then pour in saffron stock and water.
  4. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the orzo is almost tender.
  5. Add prawns and cherry tomatoes, cook for 3-4 minutes until prawns turn pink, then fold in spinach.
  6. Turn off heat, stir in parmesan and lemon juice, rest for 2 minutes, then plate with parsley and black pepper.

recipes.title: One-Pan Soy-Honey Chicken Udon with Broccolini
recipes.menu: Slippery udon, caramelized soy-honey chicken, and tender broccolini create a glossy restaurant-style bowl from one pan.
recipes.image_alt: One-Pan Soy-Honey Chicken Udon with Broccolini

sections.ingredients_html:
• 300 g chicken thigh fillet, thinly sliced
  • 1 tbsp neutral oil
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 2 tsp grated ginger
  • 1 bunch broccolini, cut into 4 cm pieces
  • 2 packet fresh udon noodles (about 400 g total)
  • 3 tbsp soy sauce
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 1 tbsp rice vinegar
  • 80 ml water
  • 1 tsp sesame oil
  • 2 spring onions, thinly sliced
  • 1 tsp toasted sesame seeds
  • Optional: chili flakes to finish
sections.method_html:
1. Heat neutral oil in a large deep frying pan over medium-high heat, add chicken, and cook for 4-5 minutes until lightly browned.
  2. Add garlic and ginger, stir for 30 seconds, then add broccolini and cook for 2 minutes.
  3. Stir in soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, and water, then simmer for 1 minute.
  4. Add udon noodles and toss for 2-3 minutes until the noodles loosen and coat in the sauce.
  5. Turn off heat, drizzle in sesame oil, and rest for 2 minutes.
  6. Serve in bowls and finish with spring onions, toasted sesame seeds, and chili flakes if using.

recipes.title: Pear, Ginger & Yuzu Soda
recipes.menu: Bright, lightly floral opener that starts the menu with crisp sweetness and citrus lift.
recipes.image_alt: Pear, Ginger & Yuzu Soda

sections.ingredients_html:
• 300 ml pear juice (chilled)
  • 1 tbsp ginger juice
  • 2 tbsp yuzu juice
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 200 ml soda water (chilled)
  • 1 cup ice cubes
  • 2 thin pear slices
sections.method_html:
1. Whisk the pear juice, ginger juice, yuzu juice, and honey in a jug until the honey dissolves.
  2. Divide the ice and pear slices between two tall glasses.
  3. Pour the pear mixture evenly into each glass and top with soda water.
  4. Optional cocktail conversion: add 30 ml dry gin per glass and stir once.
  5. Serve immediately while fully chilled and sparkling.

recipes.title: Pink Grapefruit, Thyme & Ginger Spritz
recipes.menu: Bright grapefruit with a hint of thyme and spicy ginger.
recipes.image_alt: Pink Grapefruit, Thyme & Ginger Spritz

sections.ingredients_html:
Thyme syrup

  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 2 tbsp water
  • 4-6 thyme sprigs

Spritz

  • 1/2 cup pink grapefruit juice (fresh or bottled)
  • 1 cup ginger beer
  • Soda water, to top
  • 2 tsp lemon juice (optional, for extra zip)
  • Ice

sections.method_html:
1. Make thyme syrup: simmer sugar, water, and thyme for 2 minutes, then turn off heat and steep 5 minutes. Strain and cool.

  3. Add grapefruit juice (and lemon juice if using).
  4. Top with ginger beer and a splash of soda water.
  5. Garnish with grapefruit and thyme.

recipes.title: Pomegranate, Lime & Mint Sharbat Fizz
recipes.menu: Pomegranate, lime, and mint bring a cool late-night sharbat feel that sets up the Levantine menu without any heavy prep.

  • 8 mint leaves, plus 2 extra sprigs for garnish

recipes.title: Roasted Carrots with Lemon Labneh, Pistachio Dukkah & Pomegranate Molasses
recipes.menu: Sweet roast carrots, cool lemon labneh, and nutty dukkah give the opening plate the contrast and polish of a small restaurant dish.
recipes.image_alt: Roasted Carrots with Lemon Labneh, Pistachio Dukkah & Pomegranate Molasses

sections.ingredients_html:
• 350 g carrots, halved lengthways if thick
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1/2 tsp ground cumin
  • 1/4 tsp ground coriander
  • Flaky salt
  • Black pepper
  • 160 g labneh
  • 1 tsp lemon zest
  • 2 tsp lemon juice
  • 25 g pistachios, finely chopped
  • 1 tbsp sesame seeds
  • 1/2 tsp cumin seeds
  • 1/2 tsp coriander seeds
  • 2 tsp pomegranate molasses
  • 1 tbsp mint leaves, torn
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the oven to 220 C and line a tray. Toss the carrots with the olive oil, ground cumin, ground coriander, a pinch of flaky salt, and black pepper, then roast for 20-22 minutes until tender and lightly caramelised.
  2. While the carrots roast, stir the labneh with the lemon zest, lemon juice, and a pinch of flaky salt until smooth and spreadable.
  3. Toast the pistachios, sesame seeds, cumin seeds, and coriander seeds in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until fragrant, then crush lightly with the side of a knife or a mortar and pestle.
  4. Spread the lemon labneh over a plate, pile the hot carrots on top, and drizzle over the pomegranate molasses.
  5. Finish with the pistachio dukkah and torn mint, then serve straight away.

recipes.title: Roasted Plums with Cinnamon Tortilla Crisps & Vanilla Ice Cream
recipes.menu: Warm roasted plums and crisp cinnamon tortillas finish the menu with a plated dessert that feels lighter and less familiar than the recent creamy cups.
recipes.image_alt: Roasted Plums with Cinnamon Tortilla Crisps & Vanilla Ice Cream

sections.ingredients_html:
• 3 ripe red plums, cut into wedges
  • 1 tbsp brown sugar
  • 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1 tsp lime juice
  • 2 small white corn tortillas, cut into wedges
  • 1 tbsp melted butter
  • 1 tbsp caster sugar
  • 250 ml vanilla ice cream
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • Pinch of flaky salt
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the oven to 220 C and line a small tray.
  2. Toss the plum wedges with the brown sugar, half the cinnamon, and the lime juice, then spread them over one side of the tray.
  3. Toss the tortilla wedges with the melted butter, caster sugar, the remaining cinnamon, and a tiny pinch of flaky salt, then spread them over the other side of the tray.
  4. Roast for 10-12 minutes, removing the tortilla crisps as soon as they are golden and leaving the plums in for another 4-6 minutes until glossy and just slumped.
  5. Divide the warm plums between two shallow bowls, add scoops of vanilla ice cream, and tuck in the tortilla crisps.
  6. Drizzle with honey and serve straight away.

recipes.title: Salted Caramel Pots with Hazelnut Crunch
recipes.menu: Light caramel mousse with toasted hazelnuts keeps dessert rich and bistro-friendly without bringing dark chocolate back to the table.
recipes.image_alt: Salted Caramel Pots with Hazelnut Crunch

sections.ingredients_html:
• 180 g Nestle Top 'N Fill Caramel
  • 100 g cream cheese, softened
  • 160 ml thickened cream
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
  • 30 g roasted hazelnuts, finely chopped
  • Pinch of flaky salt
sections.method_html:
1. Beat the Nestle Top 'N Fill Caramel, cream cheese, vanilla extract, and a small pinch of flaky salt together until smooth and glossy.
  2. Whip the thickened cream to soft peaks in a separate bowl.
  3. Fold two-thirds of the whipped cream through the caramel mixture until light and mousse-like.
  4. Spoon into two small glasses or ramekins and chill for 25 minutes.
  5. Top with the remaining whipped cream, the chopped roasted hazelnuts, and a tiny extra pinch of flaky salt before serving.

recipes.title: Sheet-Pan Smoky Chicken Tacos with Pickled Onion
recipes.menu: Smoky roast chicken, warm tortillas, and sharp pickled onion turn the main into an easy cantina-style assembly dinner.
recipes.image_alt: Sheet-Pan Smoky Chicken Tacos with Pickled Onion

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1/2 red onion, very thinly sliced
  • 2 tbsp lime juice
  • 1/2 tsp caster sugar
  • 1/4 tsp flaky salt
  • 350 g chicken thigh fillets (boneless, skinless), sliced
  • 1 red capsicum, thinly sliced
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 tsp smoked paprika
  • 1 tsp ground cumin
  • 1/2 tsp dried oregano
  • 1/2 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/4 tsp chilli flakes
  • 100 ml tomato passata
  • 8 small white corn tortillas
  • 80 g shredded iceberg lettuce
  • 2 tbsp chopped coriander
  • 1 lime, cut into wedges
sections.method_html:
1. Toss the sliced red onion with 1 tbsp of the lime juice, the caster sugar, and the flaky salt, then leave it to pickle while you cook the chicken.
  2. Heat the oven to 220 C and line a tray. Toss the chicken and red capsicum with the olive oil, tomato paste, smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, garlic powder, chilli flakes, and the remaining lime juice.
  3. Spread the chicken mixture over the tray and roast for 15-18 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the capsicum is tender at the edges.
  4. Pour the tomato passata over the hot tray, toss everything together, and rest for 3 minutes so the chicken picks up the smoky juices.
  5. Warm the tortillas in a dry pan or directly over a gas flame for a few seconds per side.
  6. Fill each tortilla with lettuce, smoky chicken, pickled onion, and coriander, then serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

recipes.title: Soda Fountain Cola Spiders
recipes.menu: Fizzy cola and vanilla ice cream bring instant soda-fountain nostalgia and set the diner mood from the first sip.
recipes.image_alt: Soda Fountain Cola Spiders

sections.ingredients_html:
• 500 ml chilled cola
  • 4 small scoops vanilla ice cream
  • 2 cup ice
sections.method_html:
1. Divide the ice between two tall chilled glasses.
  2. Pour the cola into the glasses, leaving a little room at the top for the foam.
  3. Add two small scoops of vanilla ice cream to each glass and let the fizz rise.
  4. Serve straight away with a straw and a long spoon.
  5. Optional cocktail conversion: add 30 ml bourbon per glass before pouring in the cola.

recipes.title: Sparkling Basil Limonata
recipes.menu: Zesty lemon and basil with gentle sweetness and a crisp sparkling finish.
recipes.image_alt: Sparkling Basil Limonata

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice
  • 2 tbsp honey or simple syrup
  • 8 fresh basil leaves, plus extra to garnish
  • 1 cup chilled apple juice
  • 1 cup chilled soda water
  • Ice cubes
  • 2 lemon wheels (for garnish)
sections.method_html:
1. In a jug, muddle basil leaves with honey and lemon juice for 20-30 seconds.
  2. Strain into the jug again for a smoother drink, pressing well to keep the basil flavor.
  3. Add apple juice and stir until combined.
  4. Fill two glasses with ice and divide the mixture evenly.
  5. Top each glass with soda water, stir gently, and garnish with a lemon wheel and basil leaf.
  6. Optional cocktail conversion: add 30 ml (1 oz) gin or vodka per glass, then stir once.

recipes.title: Sparkling Pear, Thyme & Honey Fizz
recipes.menu: Sparkling pear, honey, and thyme open the menu with a softly French bistro feel and more depth than a simple citrus spritz.
recipes.image_alt: Sparkling Pear, Thyme & Honey Fizz

sections.ingredients_html:
• 1 ripe Packham pear, peeled and chopped
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 2 sprigs thyme, plus extra to garnish
  • 2 tbsp lemon juice
  • 200 ml chilled water
  • 150 ml chilled soda water
  • Ice cubes
  • Optional: 30 ml gin per glass for cocktail serves
sections.method_html:
1. Put the honey, thyme sprigs, and 2 tbsp just-boiled water in a small cup, stir to dissolve, and leave it to steep for 5 minutes.
  2. Add the chopped pear, lemon juice, chilled water, and the strained thyme-honey syrup to a blender and blend until very smooth.
  3. Strain into a jug, pressing firmly on the solids to get the pear flavour through without a grainy texture.
  4. Fill two tall glasses with ice, divide the pear mixture between them, and top each glass with soda water.
  5. Optional cocktail conversion: add 30 ml (1 oz) gin per glass and stir.
  6. Garnish with a small thyme sprig and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Toasted Coconut Mango Rice Cups
recipes.menu: Creamy coconut rice with bright mango and toasted coconut lands as a warm-cool tropical dessert with elegant texture.
recipes.image_alt: Toasted Coconut Mango Rice Cups

sections.ingredients_html:
• 250 g microwave jasmine rice
  • 200 ml coconut cream
  • 2 tbsp caster sugar
  • 0.25 tsp sea salt
  • 1 ripe mango, diced
  • 1 tsp lime zest
  • 1 tbsp lime juice
  • 2 tbsp toasted coconut flakes
  • 1 tsp toasted sesame seeds
sections.method_html:
1. Add jasmine rice, coconut cream, caster sugar, and sea salt to a small saucepan over medium heat.
  2. Cook for 6-8 minutes, stirring often, until thick, glossy, and spoonable.
  3. Toss mango with lime zest and lime juice in a small bowl.
  4. Divide warm coconut rice between two glasses or bowls, then top with mango.
  5. Finish with toasted coconut flakes and toasted sesame seeds, then rest for 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Tray-Baked Baharat Lamb Meatballs with Herbed Couscous
recipes.menu: Spiced lamb meatballs and lemony herb couscous deliver the sense of a supper-club centerpiece while keeping the workload to one tray and one bowl.
recipes.image_alt: Tray-Baked Baharat Lamb Meatballs with Herbed Couscous

sections.ingredients_html:
• 400 g lamb mince
  • 1 small garlic clove, finely grated
  • 2 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 1 tsp ground cumin
  • 1 tsp ground coriander
  • 1/2 tsp smoked paprika
  • 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1/2 tsp flaky salt
  • Black pepper
  • 200 g cherry tomatoes
  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 120 g couscous
  • 160 ml boiling water
  • 1/2 cucumber, finely diced
  • 2 tbsp chopped mint
  • 2 tbsp chopped parsley
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 100 g thick Greek yoghurt
  • 1 tsp lemon zest
sections.method_html:
1. Heat the oven to 220 C and line a tray. Mix the lamb mince with the garlic, 2 tbsp parsley, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, cinnamon, flaky salt, and a few grinds of black pepper, then shape into 8 meatballs.
  2. Toss the cherry tomatoes with the olive oil on the tray, nestle the meatballs among them, and bake for 15-18 minutes until the lamb is cooked through and the tomatoes collapse into a quick sauce.
  3. Put the couscous in a heatproof bowl with a pinch of flaky salt, pour over the boiling water, cover, and leave for 5 minutes.
  4. Stir the Greek yoghurt with the lemon zest and half the lemon juice. Fluff the couscous with a fork, then fold through the cucumber, mint, remaining parsley, and the rest of the lemon juice.
  5. Rest the tray for 3 minutes, then spoon the herbed couscous onto plates, top with the lamb meatballs and burst tomatoes, and finish with lemon yoghurt and any tray juices.

recipes.title: Warm Beetroot, Goat's Cheese, Orange & Walnut Salad
recipes.menu: Sweet beetroot with citrus and tangy chevre, finished with toasted walnuts.
recipes.image_alt: Warm Beetroot, Goat's Cheese, Orange & Walnut Salad

sections.ingredients_html:
• 250-300 g cooked beetroot (vac-pack), cut into wedges
  • 60-80 g soft goat's cheese
  • 1 orange
  • 1 large handful rocket
  • 1/3 cup walnuts
  • 2 tsp honey
  • 1 tbsp red wine vinegar (or balsamic vinegar)
  • 2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
  • 1 tsp Dijon mustard
  • Salt and black pepper
  • Optional: 1 tbsp chopped parsley (to finish)
sections.method_html:
1. Toast walnuts in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, tossing, until fragrant.
  2. Segment the orange over a bowl so you catch the juice.
  3. Whisk orange juice (about 2 tbsp), honey, vinegar, olive oil, Dijon, salt, and pepper to make a dressing.
  4. Warm beetroot wedges in the same pan for 1-2 minutes, just to take the chill off.
  5. Toss rocket with half the dressing and arrange on two plates.
  6. Add warm beetroot and orange segments.
  7. Crumble over goat's cheese, scatter walnuts, and spoon over remaining dressing.
  8. Finish with black pepper and parsley, if using.

recipes.title: Warm Honeyed Pears with Vanilla Ricotta & Walnut Crunch
recipes.menu: Warm spiced pears, cool vanilla ricotta, and crisp walnut crunch finish the meal with elegant contrast.
recipes.image_alt: Warm Honeyed Pears with Vanilla Ricotta & Walnut Crunch

sections.ingredients_html:
• 2 ripe pears, cored and sliced
  • 1 tbsp unsalted butter
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 120 g ricotta
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 20 g walnuts, finely chopped
  • 1 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tsp lemon zest
  • 1 pinch sea salt
sections.method_html:
1. Toast walnuts, sugar, and sea salt in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant, then transfer to a plate.
  2. In a small bowl, whisk ricotta and vanilla until smooth.
  3. Melt butter in the same skillet, add pears, honey, and cinnamon, and cook for 6-8 minutes until glossy and tender.
  4. Spoon vanilla ricotta into shallow bowls and top with warm pears and pan syrup.
  5. Finish with walnut crunch and lemon zest just before serving.

recipes.title: Watermelon, Lime & Mint Agua Fresca
recipes.menu: A chilled watermelon agua fresca opens the menu with clean fruit, sharp lime, and a fresh mint lift instead of another spritz.
recipes.image_alt: Watermelon, Lime & Mint Agua Fresca

sections.ingredients_html:
• 450 g seedless watermelon, cubed
  • 2 tbsp lime juice
  • 1 tbsp caster sugar
  • 8 mint leaves, plus extra to garnish
  • 250 ml chilled water
  • Pinch of flaky salt
  • Ice cubes
  • Optional: 30 ml blanco tequila per glass for cocktail serves
sections.method_html:
1. Add the watermelon, lime juice, caster sugar, mint leaves, chilled water, and flaky salt to a blender and blend until very smooth.
  2. Taste and add a little more lime if the melon is very sweet, then strain through a fine sieve if you want a smoother finish.
  3. Fill two short glasses with ice and divide the agua fresca between them.
  4. Optional cocktail conversion: add 30 ml blanco tequila per glass and stir.
  5. Garnish with extra mint and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Whipped Feta Artichoke Toasts with Lemon
recipes.menu: Creamy whipped feta and warm lemony artichokes on crisp toast for a savory first bite.
recipes.image_alt: Whipped Feta Artichoke Toasts with Lemon

sections.ingredients_html:
• 120 g feta
  • 80 g ricotta
  • 1 tsp lemon zest
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1 small garlic clove, finely grated
  • 1 tbsp olive oil, plus extra to drizzle
  • 1 cup jarred artichoke hearts, drained and chopped
  • 1 tbsp chopped parsley or basil
  • 4 slices sourdough
  • Freshly cracked black pepper
sections.method_html:
1. Toast the sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
  2. In a bowl, mash feta, ricotta, lemon zest, and half the lemon juice until creamy and spreadable.
  3. Warm olive oil in a small pan over medium heat, then cook artichokes with garlic for 2-3 minutes.
  4. Add remaining lemon juice and parsley, toss for 30 seconds, then turn off the heat.
  5. Spread whipped feta on the toasts, spoon warm artichokes over the top, and finish with black pepper and a light olive oil drizzle.
